methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Emelody is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jon Thomas that has raised $10M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private robotics companies (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public comparable, though narrow peer matches within identical stage and valuation tier proved limited.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable here, anchoring to the $10M total capital raised as a proxy for prior valuation and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $12M. The Peer-Median approach could not be reliably applied; while 11 same-sector peers exist in the Robotics & Manufacturing universe, filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier produced fewer than three comparable datapoints, making a median-based estimate unreliable and potentially misleading.

The triangulated valuation range is $10M (low) to $14M (high), with a median point estimate of $12M. This analysis is constrained by the absence of sufficiently granular public and private comps at identical stage and scale. Key uncertainties include Emelody's specific revenue, margin profile, and technology differentiation relative to the broader peer set—factors that would typically narrow or shift this range materially if disclosed.
